Radiant Rest: A Yoga Nidra Meditation Series with Carla Villa
May 21, 6pm: Join us for a gentle, restorative meditation series inspired by Radiant Rest. Together, we’ll explore the ancient practice of yoga nidra, a deeply restorative meditation that invites you to rest in spacious awareness while reconnecting with your innate wisdom and clarity.
Library CLOSED for Memorial Day
The Library will be closed May 23-25 in observance of Memorial Day.

pass_page.policy_text:
To limit museum types being displayed, check the corresponding categories:
To select a museum, click “Details/Reserve”, then select which Pass to reserve.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "308 Congress Street, Boston, MA 02210","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-boston-childrens-museum_3_md.jpg","telephone": "617-426-6500","name": "Boston Children&apos;s Museum","url": "","description": "Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers&apos; Bureau, the Boston Children&apos;s Museum is the second oldest children&apos;s museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children."} Boston Children's Museum 308 Congress Street, Boston, MA 02210617-426-6500http://www.bostonchildrensmuseum.org/[Details/Reserve]
Boston Children's Museum Founded in 1913 by the Science Teachers' Bureau, the Boston Children's Museum is the second oldest children's museum in the world and one of the few that maintain a collection. Designed for both children and families, the Museum exhibits focus on science, culture, environmental awareness, health & fitness, and the arts, emphasizing hands-on engagement and learning through experience, and employing play as a tool to spark the inherent creativity, curiosity, and imagination of children.
Categories: Children, Crafts, Family, Technology
Pass Type: Printable/Digital Coupon Pass (link delivered by email)
Pass Benefits This coupon pass allows half-price entry to the Children's Museum for up to 4 people. Passes will be emailed to reserving patrons, without the need to come to the library to pick up the pass! Please contact the library to reserve this pass if you do not have an email address.
The Children's Museum is open Wednesday through Monday.
All ticketing is done online, with two time slots to reserve: 9:00am-12:00pm, and 1:30-4:30pm. The Children’s Museum is currently operating under full capacity. Upon reserving a library pass, make sure to reserve your timed ticket right away on the Children’s Museum website to ensure availability. Select “Discount Program Admission Tickets” and “Half Price Library Timed Ticket.” You will need to present your ticket and museum pass upon entry. Patrons with questions about the online ordering system should contact Orders@BostonChildrensMuseum.org
Reserve a Pass Reserve a Pass

Transportation to the islands is by ferry and, depending on the location, your own boat. Please refer to their website for the most up-to-date information about what is open and how to get there."} Boston Harbor Island Ferry 15 State Street, Suite 1100, Boston, MA 02109617-223-8666http://www.bostonharborislands.org/[Details/Reserve]
Boston Harbor Island Ferry The largest recreational open space in Eastern Massachusetts, the Boston Harbor Islands National and State Park is comprised of 34 islands and mainland parks that deliver access to historic sites and provide opportunities to hike and camp. Transportation to the islands is by ferry and, depending on the location, your own boat. Please refer to their website for the most up-to-date information about what is open and how to get there.
Categories: Family, Nature, Recreation
Pass Type: Coupon Pass (must be picked up from the branch, but does not require returning)
Pass Benefits Using this discount through the library, you receive 50% off tickets to the Boston Harbor Islands ferry, taking you to Georges Island (from Long Wharf, Boston), Spectacle Island (from Long Wharf, Boston), and Peddocks Island (from Hingham) in Boston Harbor.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "83 Pine Street, Unit F, Peabody, MA 01960","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-the-childrens-piazza_220_md.jpg","telephone": "978-817-2809","name": "The Children&apos;s Piazza","url": "","description": "Located in Peabody, MA, The Children&apos;s Piazza is a space is designed for young children&mdash;aged 0 to 6&mdash;to learn through unstructured play. Organized to optimize social, cognitive and motor development, areas include a large room with a climbing structure, slides, and swings; an arts and crafts room; a room with a train table; a lunch area with appropriately sized furniture; a glow-in-the-dark planet room; among others. There is a coffee shop and free Wi-Fi on the premises. Please check the website for the requirements of entry."} The Children's Piazza 83 Pine Street, Unit F, Peabody, MA 01960978-817-2809https://www.thechildrenspiazza.com/[Details/Reserve]
The Children's Piazza Located in Peabody, MA, The Children's Piazza is a space is designed for young children—aged 0 to 6—to learn through unstructured play. Organized to optimize social, cognitive and motor development, areas include a large room with a climbing structure, slides, and swings; an arts and crafts room; a room with a train table; a lunch area with appropriately sized furniture; a glow-in-the-dark planet room; among others. There is a coffee shop and free Wi-Fi on the premises. Please check the website for the requirements of entry.
Categories: Children, Family
Pass Benefits  The pass to the Children's Piazza provides 50% off admission for one family/same household per day. The pass does not cover members of the group who are not immediate family/household.
The email confirmation sent to you serves as your discount pass. Please present this pass (either printed or on your mobile device) upon entry to the Children's Piazza. Pass is only valid on date reserved. Reservations always recommended! 
Pass donated by the the Friends of the Topsfield Library; funded in part by a generous grant from the Institution for Savings.
The email confirmation sent to you serves as your discount pass. Please present this pass (either printed or on your mobile device) upon entry to the Children's Piazza. Pass is only valid on date reserved.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "25 Evans Way, Boston, MA 02115","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-gardner-museum_14_md.jpg","telephone": "617-566-1401","name": "Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum","url": "","description": "The Gardner Museum houses the collection of Isabella Stewart Gardner, a local patron of the arts who died in 1924, in a building she commissioned to look like a 15th century Venetian palazzo. The center of the original building showcases courtyard plantings that reflect the seasons as they evolve throughout the year. In addition to her collection, which remains displayed exactly as she arranged it per her will, there are changing exhibitions, concerts, lectures, family events, and an artist-in-residence program. In 2012, the museum opened an addition designed by Renzo Piano that houses a space for their concert series. "} Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum 25 Evans Way, Boston, MA 02115617-566-1401http://www.gardnermuseum.org/home/[Details/Reserve]
Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum The Gardner Museum houses the collection of Isabella Stewart Gardner, a local patron of the arts who died in 1924, in a building she commissioned to look like a 15th century Venetian palazzo. The center of the original building showcases courtyard plantings that reflect the seasons as they evolve throughout the year. In addition to her collection, which remains displayed exactly as she arranged it per her will, there are changing exhibitions, concerts, lectures, family events, and an artist-in-residence program. In 2012, the museum opened an addition designed by Renzo Piano that houses a space for their concert series.
Categories: Art, Family, Music
Pass Benefits This pass is an online promo code; nothing needs to be picked up from the library.
This pass allows up to 4 people to visit the Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum for $5.00 each. To reserve your tickets online, visit the following website: https://tnew.gardnermuseum.org/events. Enter the provided promo code (sent to your email address once pass is reserved) in the box at the top of the screen. Then, select visit date and time to enter. Add the desired number of tickets to the basket (note: discount only works for up to 4 Adult-priced tickets and any additional ticket will be normally priced). Children under 18 are always free and should be reserved as child tickets. Discount is applied only at checkout. Once you login and create an account with the Gardner Museum, you will be able to finish checking out. This library pass does not guarantee a reservation to the Museum.
Please present your reserved timed museum admission tickets, and the email sent to confirm your pass reservation (either on your mobile device or printed), while checking in to visit the Isabella Stewart Gardner Museum.
{"@context": "http://schema.org","@type": "CivicStructure","address": "Columbia Point, Boston, MA 02125","logo": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/ai-assets/museum-logos/ma-jfk-library-and-museum_15_md.jpg","telephone": "617-514-1600","name": "John F. Kennedy Library &amp; Museum","url": "","description": "The John F. Kennedy Presidential Library and Museum, dedicated to the memory of the nation&apos;s thirty-fifth president, is located on a ten-acre park which overlooks the sea in an building designed by I.M. Pei. The Museum portrays the life, leadership, and legacy of President Kennedy, conveys his enthusiasm for politics and public service, and illustrates the nature of the office of the President, all through permanent and changing exhibits. The Library is available to students and scholars to conduct research using its collection of historical materials chronicling mid-20th century politics and the life and administration of the institution&apos;s namesake."} John F. Kennedy Library & Museum Columbia Point, Boston, MA 02125617-514-1600https://www.jfklibrary.org/[Details/Reserve]
John F. Kennedy Library & Museum The John F. Kennedy Presidential Library and Museum, dedicated to the memory of the nation's thirty-fifth president, is located on a ten-acre park which overlooks the sea in an building designed by I.M. Pei. The Museum portrays the life, leadership, and legacy of President Kennedy, conveys his enthusiasm for politics and public service, and illustrates the nature of the office of the President, all through permanent and changing exhibits. The Library is available to students and scholars to conduct research using its collection of historical materials chronicling mid-20th century politics and the life and administration of the institution's namesake.
